title: Dactylorhiza × claudiopolitana
intro: Dactylorhiza × claudiopolitana is a hybrid orchid species, resulting from the cross between Dactylorhiza sambucina and Dactylorhiza maculata. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may aid in wound healing and reduce inflammation. Bioactive constituents of Dactylorhiza × claudiopolitana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is often used in traditional herbal medicine and can be found in various herbal preparations, including tinctures and infusions, as well as in dried root form for use in teas and capsules.
taxonomy: Dactylorhiza × claudiopolitana is a hybrid orchid species resulting from the cross between Dactylorhiza iberica and Dactylorhiza sambucina. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae and is native to parts of Eastern Europe. The hybrid nature of Dactylorhiza × claudiopolitana makes it distinct from its parent species. As a hybrid, Dactylorhiza × claudiopolitana is not considered a distinct species in its own right, but rather an intermediate between its parent species. The classification of Dactylorhiza × claudiopolitana is still a subject of study and debate among botanists.
